content.problemStatement:
An array BBB of length MMM is called good if 2⋅Bi<Bi+12 \cdot B_i < B_{i + 1}2⋅Bi​<Bi+1​ for all 1≤i<M1 \le i < M1≤i<M. For example, [1,3][1, 3][1,3] is good, but [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] are not.

For a **sorted** array BBB, we define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the length of the longest good subsequence of BBB. For example, for the array B=[1,2,3]B = [1, 2, 3]B=[1,2,3], f(B)=2f(B) = 2f(B)=2 as obtained by the subsequence [1,3][1, 3][1,3].

You are given a **sorted** array AAA of length NNN.

Find the sum of f(C)f(C)f(C) for all CCC which are non-empty subsequences of AAA. Note that are exactly 2N−12^N - 12N−1 such possible arrays CCC (if one array appears multiple times, it should be counted multiple times).

Since the answer may be large, find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, - the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(C)f(C)f(C) for all 2N−12^N - 12N−1 subsequences of AAA modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
